stage_prompt.name: Cutover — Repoint All Surfaces to devplatform

comprehension_gate.rule: MANDATORY FIRST ACTION. Present structured summary. STOP and WAIT for Lead Architect approval.

vikunja_access_protocol.rationale: Stage 1 Anomaly A5 -- Executor surfaces vary in MCP-tool exposure; REST is documented as functionally equivalent fallback
vikunja_access_protocol.intro: Stage operations that read or write Vikunja MAY use either Model Context Protocol (MCP) tools OR direct Representational State Transfer (REST) against the Vikunja API. Both paths are byte-equivalent at the Vikunja server. Preference: MCP when the Executor surface exposes mcp__vikunja__* tools; REST is the documented fallback when MCP is not surfaced.
vikunja_access_protocol.preferred: Tool prefixes: mcp__vikunja__list_projects, mcp__vikunja__create_project, mcp__vikunja__list_tasks, mcp__vikunja__create_task, mcp__vikunja__update_task, mcp__vikunja__add_task_comment, mcp__vikunja__list_labels, mcp__vikunja__add_label_to_task. Precondition: Vikunja v2.3.0 server running on http://localhost:3456 (start via Start-Process C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\tools\vikunja\vikunja-v2.3.0-windows-4.0-amd64.exe; poll /api/v1/info before first call).
vikunja_access_protocol.fallback: Direct REST via Invoke-RestMethod. Authentication flow: $cred = Get-Content C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\.vscode\mcp.json -Raw | ConvertFrom-Json $pass = $cred.mcpServers.vikunja.env.VIKUNJA_PASS # read-only; do NOT write back $body = @{ username='blarai'; password=$pass } | ConvertTo-Json $token = (Invoke-RestMethod -Uri 'http://localhost:3456/api/v1/login' -Method POST ` -Body $body -ContentType 'application/json').token $headers = @{ Authorization = "Bearer $token" } # Subsequent calls: GET/PUT/POST against /api/v1/projects, /api/v1/tasks, # /api/v1/labels, etc.
vikunja_access_protocol.credential_source: Single canonical source for Vikunja credentials: C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\.vscode\mcp.json key path mcpServers.vikunja.env.VIKUNJA_PASS. Both MCP path and REST path read this same value. The User-scoped VIKUNJA_PASS env var (persisted at Stage 0.0.5) is used by the MCP server process itself; for direct PowerShell scripts, read from the .vscode/mcp.json file as shown above.
vikunja_access_protocol.safety: Neither path satisfies Master Plan section D.1 enforcement -- D.1 is enforced by tools/_vikunja_client.py (created in Stage 2.1) for fleet RUNTIME tools, not for stage-execution operations. Stage operations using either path must still pass explicit project_id when the operation is project-scoped.

v2_updates.note: Additive to v1 work_items 4.0–4.13. Several items WRAP existing destructive blocks with the fleet-pause/resume triplet — mind the ordering.

item.name: Re-verify worktrees-empty gate (re-checks Stage 0.5.5)
item.description: Stage 0 verified worktrees are empty. Re-verify here because Stage 4 destructive ops will fail catastrophically with an active worktree on the same branch.
item.on_failure: HALT. Resolve worktree (commit + `git worktree remove`) and re-run.

item.name: Re-confirm fleet is paused (defense-in-depth)
item.description: Stage 0.0 paused the fleet. Re-confirm immediately before destructive cutover — someone may have resumed manually.
item.command: python -c "import json; print('PAUSED' if json.load(open('tools/autonomy_budget/state.json'))['fleet_paused'] else 'RUNNING -- HALT')"
item.on_failure: Re-pause via state.pause_fleet(...) BEFORE proceeding to 4.1. Commit the re-pause.

item.name: Wake-template path-rewrite scan
item.description: v1 item 4.7 rewrites scheduled-task XML paths but does NOT touch wake templates. Wake templates are read by wake_launcher.ps1 at fire time and contain hardcoded BlarAI tool paths.
item.preserve: References to BlarAI as the TARGET PROJECT (e.g., -BlarAIRoot 'C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI') MUST stay. Only platform-tool path refs are rewritten.

item.name: PS1 line-by-line path-rewrite scan (5 scripts)
item.description: **G-1 fix.** Five PS1 scripts under `tools/scheduled-tasks/` carry a hardcoded `[string]$RepoRoot = 'C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI'` (or `$BlarAIRoot` post-Stage-2). After Stage 2.4.v2 / 2.5.v2 add the `-BlarAIRoot` parameter, every remaining literal must be either (a) the parameter default, or (b) a comment/docstring referencing the target project. Anything else is a bug. **Correct path: `tools\scheduled-tasks\` — none of these have ever lived under `tools\fleet_ops\`.**
item.expected: Each remaining match MUST be either (a) a `$BlarAIRoot` parameter default, or (b) a comment / docstring referencing the target project. Anything else is a bug — fix the script in BlarAI/tools/, recopy to devplatform, re-run this scan.

item.name: Confirm Vikunja autostart form (G-3)
item.description: **G-3 fix.** `vikunja-autostart` exists in two possible forms on this host: (a) a Windows scheduled task at `\` (root TaskPath, NOT `\BlarAI\`), and/or (b) a `.lnk` shortcut in `shell:startup`. Stage 4.7 only rewrites tasks under `\BlarAI\`; Stage 4.9 only handles `.lnk` files. Without this check, Vikunja can re-spawn from BlarAI on next reboot, racing the devplatform copy and corrupting the SQLite DB.
item.expected: One of: (a) at least one autostart entry exists and now points at devplatform, or (b) Lead Architect explicitly confirms manual-start is acceptable.
item.on_failure: If a BlarAI-referencing task is found, repoint it manually (use the same XML-rewrite pattern as 4.7) before resuming the fleet in 4.13.5.

item.name: Standardize task filter and confirm count is 13
item.description: v1 item 4.8 enables tasks one-by-one. The total count is **13** (live fleet at TaskPath `\BlarAI\`). Earlier v2 drafts incorrectly inflated this to 14; corrected. Use `-TaskPath '\BlarAI\'` filter — name-only `BlarAI*` filters return zero because the live tasks use Title Case Spaces (e.g. `Wake SDO`, NOT `BlarAI_wake_sdo`).

item.name: Resume fleet after successful cutover (LAST destructive-section action)
item.description: After all 4.x items pass and end-to-end smoke test confirms devplatform is authoritative, RESUME the fleet so it can fire from the new location.
item.api_gotcha: Function name is `resume_fleet`, NOT `unpause_fleet`. AttributeError if you use the wrong name.
item.then: git add tools/autonomy_budget/state.json; git commit -m "chore(ops): unpause fleet -- platform separation v2 cutover done"
item.on_failure: If smoke test failed, do NOT resume. Roll back via Stage 4 rollback. Fleet stays paused until rollback or fix.

stage_prompt.stage_importance:
**This is the dangerous stage.** Three external surfaces switch from BlarAI-backed platform to devplatform-backed platform: (a) Claude Desktop / VS Code / Claude Code MCP configs, (b) Vikunja server autostart location, (c) Windows Scheduled Tasks (re-enabled with new paths).
Must be executed in a **single continuous session** — do NOT stop in the middle. Between the moment BlarAI copies stop and devplatform copies start, the fleet is offline. Keep the window short.
stage_prompt.objective: Switch all external surfaces to point at `C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform\tools\`. After this stage, closing the BlarAI copy would not affect fleet operation. The BlarAI platform-tool files still exist on disk but are dormant (Stage 5 deletes them).

preconditions.check: Stage 3 complete — devplatform copy smoke-tested successfully.
Vikunja server is currently stopped (from Stage 0.4).
Claude Desktop, VS Code, Claude Code all closed before starting.

item.name: Close all AI development surfaces
item.description: Close Claude Desktop, all VS Code windows, Claude Code terminals. Verify no python processes from tools/vikunja_mcp/ are running. NOTE: Get-Process has no CommandLine property — must use Get-CimInstance Win32_Process for command-line filtering.

item.name: Migrate Vikunja SQLite DB to devplatform
item.description: Vikunja stores its DB under `tools/vikunja/`. This data (projects, tasks, comments) is irreplaceable. Defensive flow: (1) Confirm Vikunja is fully stopped before any file motion. (2) Refuse to clobber any existing destination DB. (3) Capture SHA256 before move, compare after move, abort on mismatch. (4) Keep a side-copy backup at the source path so rollback is one Move-Item away. (5) Best-effort SQLite integrity check if sqlite3.exe is on PATH.

item.name: Start Vikunja from devplatform
item.expected: HTTP 200 from Vikunja info endpoint within 30 seconds.
item.rollback_if_fails: Stop the process. Run the rollback_one_liner from item 4.1 to restore BlarAI DB. Restart Vikunja from BlarAI. Abort stage.

item.name: Verify tasks and projects intact in Vikunja
item.description: Requires $env:VIKUNJA_TOKEN to be set in current session. Obtain a token via the Vikunja UI (http://localhost:3456 → User settings → API tokens → create with read access on Projects+Tasks) BEFORE running Stage 4. Set with: $env:VIKUNJA_TOKEN = "<token>".
item.expected: Shows BlarAI project and DevPlatform-Meta project with correct IDs.

file.action: Update the vikunja MCP server entry. Change path from BlarAI/tools/vikunja_mcp to devplatform/tools/vikunja_mcp. Add a second workspace entry per §F (both registered alongside). **F-1 fix**: replace the `VIKUNJA_PASS` literal value with the string `${env:VIKUNJA_PASS}` (Claude Desktop expands env-var references on launch).
file.verify_no_literal: Select-String -Path "$env:APPDATA\Claude\claude_desktop_config.json" -Pattern 'VIKUNJA_PASS.*[A-Za-z0-9]{16,}' — expected NO match (only `${env:VIKUNJA_PASS}` reference should remain).

item.name: Update VS Code Copilot MCP config
item.description: BlarAI's `.vscode/mcp.json` currently points at BlarAI/tools/vikunja_mcp. Update to point at devplatform, BUT keep BlarAI's copy working as a fallback during the soak period. devplatform/.vscode/mcp.json is authoritative. **F-1 fix**: replace the `VIKUNJA_PASS` literal with the string `${env:VIKUNJA_PASS}` (VS Code MCP loader expands at server-launch time).

files_touched.file: C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\.vscode\mcp.json → point to devplatform path; replace VIKUNJA_PASS literal with `${env:VIKUNJA_PASS}`
C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform\.vscode\mcp.json → already created in Stage 3.6 with env-var pattern, verify correct

item.name: Update Claude Code project-scoped .mcp.json
item.description: BlarAI's `.mcp.json` (used by Claude Code CLI). Repoint vikunja entry to devplatform. devplatform gets its own `.mcp.json` from Stage 3.6. **F-1 fix**: replace the `VIKUNJA_PASS` literal with the string `${env:VIKUNJA_PASS}` (Claude Code expands env-var refs at MCP server spawn).
item.smoke_test: After all three configs (4.4/4.5/4.6) are rewritten, restart any one client (e.g., open a new VS Code window in BlarAI workspace) and confirm Vikunja MCP connects. If it fails to authenticate, the User env var from Stage 0.0.5 is not visible to the new process — verify with `[Environment]::GetEnvironmentVariable('VIKUNJA_PASS','User')` in a new shell.

item.name: Start bridge daemon from devplatform BEFORE re-enabling scheduled tasks
item.description: **Ordering bug fix (was item 4.10 in v1).** The Vikunja bridge daemon (file-based MCP sync for sandbox agents) MUST be running from devplatform before any scheduled task fires — otherwise wake_* tasks that depend on bridge sync will fail their first run after re-enable. Start it here, then verify in 4.10.
item.expected: Bridge process visible with devplatform in CommandLine.

item.name: Rewrite scheduled-task XML definitions with new paths
item.description: Read each exported XML from `C:\Users\mrbla\backups\scheduled_tasks_export\`, rewrite the `<Arguments>` and `<WorkingDirectory>` entries to reference `C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform\` instead of `C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\`. Tasks must also pass `--project-root C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI --project-id $BLARAI_PID` (so they continue operating on the BlarAI project, but from the devplatform toolchain). Re-import updated XMLs.

commands.cmd: # For each exported task, replace paths AND inject CLI args, then re-register. # NOTE: Export-ScheduledTask emits XML with a UTF-16 BOM. Register-ScheduledTask -Xml requires a clean string # — the BOM is stripped explicitly below. The loop also wraps each task in try/catch so a single failure # doesn't leave the rest of the fleet half-migrated. $errors = @() Get-ChildItem C:\Users\mrbla\backups\scheduled_tasks_export\*.xml | ForEach-Object { $name = $_.BaseName try { $xml = Get-Content $_.FullName -Raw # Strip UTF-16 BOM if present (Export-ScheduledTask default) $xml = $xml -replace "^\uFEFF", "" $xml = $xml -replace [regex]::Escape("C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\tools"), "C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform\tools" $xml = $xml -replace [regex]::Escape("C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\.venv"), "C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform\.venv" # G-2 fix: rewrite bare C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI too. # Without this, `python -m tools.X` invocations resolve against BlarAI's dormant tools tree # — the Stage 4.8 smoke test passes false-green, and the fleet breaks at Stage 5 deletion # (~24-72h post-cutover) when the dormant copy disappears. Six observability tasks rely on # this: Daily Digest, Weekly Summary, Dashboard Maintainer, Welcome Back Poll, Credentials # Rotation Reminder, Gate Stale Cleaner. Conservative regex: only the bare BlarAI root # inside a WorkingDirectory element (does NOT touch -BlarAIRoot args, which legitimately # point at the target project). $xml = $xml -replace '(?<=)C:\\Users\\mrbla\\BlarAI(?=)', 'C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform' # Inject --project-root / --project-id into the element so tasks keep operating on BlarAI. # Pattern is conservative: only inject if the placeholders aren't already present. if ($xml -notmatch '--project-root') { $xml = $xml -replace '()', " --project-root C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI --project-id $BLARAI_PID`$1" } $updatedPath = "C:\Users\mrbla\backups\scheduled_tasks_export\updated\$name.xml" New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path (Split-Path $updatedPath) -Force | Out-Null # Write WITHOUT BOM (UTF-8 no-BOM) to preserve for inspection [System.IO.File]::WriteAllText($updatedPath, $xml, (New-Object System.Text.UTF8Encoding $false)) # Unregister old, register new — leave Disabled (item 4.8 enables one at a time) Unregister-ScheduledTask -TaskName $name -TaskPath '\BlarAI\' -Confirm:$false -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue Register-ScheduledTask -Xml $xml -TaskName $name -TaskPath '\BlarAI\' | Out-Null Disable-ScheduledTask -TaskName $name -TaskPath '\BlarAI\' | Out-Null Write-Host "OK : $name" } catch { $errors += [PSCustomObject]@{ Task = $name; Error = $_.Exception.Message } Write-Host "FAIL: $name — $($_.Exception.Message)" -ForegroundColor Red } } if ($errors.Count -gt 0) { $errors | Format-Table -AutoSize throw "$($errors.Count) task(s) failed to re-register. STOP and investigate before item 4.8." }

item.manual_review: After automated replace, Lead Architect should eyeball 2-3 updated XMLs to confirm paths look right before re-enabling.

item.name: Enable scheduled tasks one at a time, verify each
item.description: Do NOT bulk-enable. Enable one task, wait for it to actually finish (LastTaskResult ≠ 267009 = "task is currently running"), then check exit code, then move to the next. Auto-disable on failure so subsequent tasks don't fire blindly.

order.task: 1. Wake SDO (lightest, safest to test first)
6. ... remaining (Sprint Auditor, Toast Watchdog, Welcome Back Poll, Weekly Summary, Dashboard Maintainer, Agents Cadence Monitor, Credentials Rotation Reminder, Escalation Watchdog)

item.expected: LastTaskResult = 0 for each task. Function auto-disables on failure to fail-closed.

item.name: Update Windows Startup Vikunja shortcut
item.description: If a Vikunja autostart shortcut exists in `shell:startup`, repoint TargetPath and WorkingDirectory via WScript.Shell COM (the .lnk format is binary OLE — cannot be edited as text).
item.command: $startup = "$env:APPDATA\Microsoft\Windows\Start Menu\Programs\Startup" $candidates = Get-ChildItem $startup -Filter "*.lnk" -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue $sh = New-Object -ComObject WScript.Shell foreach ($lnk in $candidates) { $sc = $sh.CreateShortcut($lnk.FullName) if ($sc.TargetPath -match "BlarAI\\tools\\vikunja") { $oldTarget = $sc.TargetPath $oldWd = $sc.WorkingDirectory # Backup the old .lnk before mutation Copy-Item $lnk.FullName "$($lnk.FullName).pre_stage4_bak" -Force $sc.TargetPath = $sc.TargetPath -replace [regex]::Escape("C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI"), "C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform" $sc.WorkingDirectory = $sc.WorkingDirectory -replace [regex]::Escape("C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI"), "C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform" $sc.Save() Write-Host "Updated $($lnk.Name):" Write-Host " Target: $oldTarget -> $($sc.TargetPath)" Write-Host " WorkDir: $oldWd -> $($sc.WorkingDirectory)" } }

item.name: Re-verify bridge daemon health (started in 4.6.5)
item.description: The bridge daemon was started in item 4.6.5 BEFORE scheduled tasks were re-enabled. This step re-verifies it is still alive and not crashed by any task that exercised it during 4.8.
item.command: Get-CimInstance Win32_Process -Filter "Name = 'python.exe'" | Where-Object { $_.CommandLine -match 'devplatform.*tools\.vikunja_mcp\.bridge' } | Select-Object ProcessId, CommandLine
item.expected: One row showing the bridge process. If absent, inspect bridge logs and restart via 4.6.5 command before 4.11 smoke test.

item.name: End-to-end smoke test from a real agent
item.description: Open a fresh Claude Desktop session. Ask it to list Vikunja projects via MCP. Confirm it returns results. This validates Claude Desktop → updated config → devplatform MCP server → Vikunja is end-to-end working.

item.name: Commit both repos
item.command: cd C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI; git add .vscode/mcp.json .mcp.json; git commit -m "Stage 4: repoint MCP configs to devplatform"; cd C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform; git add .; git commit -m "Stage 4: cutover — devplatform now authoritative"

item.name: Update STATUS.md
item.description: Mark Stage 4 COMPLETE. Record: Vikunja DB hash (to detect corruption later), list of re-enabled tasks and their LastTaskResult, end-to-end smoke test evidence.

steps.s: Leave scheduled tasks Disabled. Abort Stage 4.

trigger_level_2.label: MCP config update breaks Claude Desktop (4.4)

steps.s: Do NOT enable subsequent tasks until root cause found.
Check Task Scheduler event log for the specific error.
Stop Vikunja, bridge daemon, disable all scheduled tasks.
Restore all MCP configs from backups.
Move Vikunja DB back to BlarAI location.
Start Vikunja from BlarAI. Re-enable scheduled tasks (they still point at BlarAI from Stage 0 exports).
On BlarAI: git revert the Stage 4 commit.
Fleet should now be back to pre-Stage-4 state, running from BlarAI, with devplatform dormant.

verification_commands_for_lead_architect.cmd: Get-CimInstance Win32_Process -Filter "Name = 'python.exe'" | Where-Object { $_.CommandLine -match 'devplatform.*vikunja_mcp' } | Select-Object ProcessId, CommandLine

exit_criteria.criterion: Vikunja DB migrated (only one copy exists — in devplatform).
All fleet scheduled tasks re-enabled AND last-run result = 0.
All MCP configs repointed to devplatform.
End-to-end smoke test from Claude Desktop passes.
STATUS.md updated with Vikunja DB hash and task statuses.
